creator: dryice@dryice.name
text:
Attached is the shar file for devel/py-protocols-devel. The
	stable version is in the ports tree. This snapshot is the
	latest from the author. And has been on the net for over a
	year.

	I made this -devel port for the (coming) TurboGears-devel port.

Fix: --- attached file begins here ---


--- attached file ends here -----mdNCxqPG6UIsUna0l3KTpitaPHhUy9zgab2PdUWFTkP5tHVJ
Content-Type: text/plain; name="file.shar"
Content-Transfer-Encoding: 7bit
Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.shar"

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/
#	/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/distinfo
#	/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/Makefile
#	/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-descr
#	/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-plist
#
echo c - /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/
mkdir -p /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/ > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/distinfo << 'END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/distinfo'
XMD5 (PyProtocols-1.0a0dev_r2082.zip) = 11c5871560b65cf72b45845013297b94
XSHA256 (PyProtocols-1.0a0dev_r2082.zip) = fb8b9a3db25e0b447fbf327c26b859368cefdc14438b9752ab248b788f119016
XSIZE (PyProtocols-1.0a0dev_r2082.zip) = 124061
END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/distinfo
echo x - /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/Makefile << 'END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	py-protocols-devel
X# Date created:		15 December 2006
X# Whom:			Dryice Liu	<dryice@dryice.name>
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	PyProtocols
XPORTVERSION=	1.0.a0.r2082
XCATEGORIES=	devel python
XMASTER_SITES=	http://peak.telecommunity.com/snapshots/ \
X		http://dryice.name/computer/FreeBSD/distfiles/
XPKGNAMEPREFIX=	${PYTHON_PKGNAMEPREFIX}
XDISTNAME=	${PORTNAME}-1.0a0dev_r2082
X
XMAINTAINER=	dryice@dryice.name
XCOMMENT=	Protocol Definition, Declaration, and Adaptation for Python
X
XBUILD_DEPENDS=	easy_install:${PORTSDIR}/devel/py-setuptools
XRUN_DEPENDS=	${BUILD_DEPENDS}
X
XUSE_ZIP=	yes
XUSE_PYTHON=	2.3+
XUSE_PYDISTUTILS=	yes
XPYPROTOCOLSVER?=	1.0a0
XPYTHONVER?=	${_PYTHON_VERSION}
XEGGOSREL!=	uname -r
XPLIST_SUB=	PYPROTOCOLSVER=${PYPROTOCOLSVER} \
X		PYTHONVER=${PYTHONVER} \
X		EGGOSREL=${EGGOSREL} \
X		ARCH=${ARCH}
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/Makefile
echo x - /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-descr << 'END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-descr'
XThis package builds on the object adaptation protocol presented in PEP
X246 to make it easier for component authors, framework suppliers, and
Xother developers to:
X
X    * Specify what behavior a component requires or provides
X
X    * Specify how to adapt the interface provided by one component to
X      that required by another
X
X    * Specify how to adapt objects of a particular type or class (even
X      built-in types) to a particular required interface
X
X    * Automatically adapt a supplied object to a required interface,
X      and
X
X    * Do all of the above, even when the components or frameworks
X      involved were not written to take advantage of this package, and
X      even if the frameworks have different mechanisms for defining
X      interfaces.
X
X
XAuthor:	Phillip J. Eby
XWWW:	http://peak.telecommunity.com/PyProtocols.html
END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-descr
echo x - /usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-plist << 'END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-plist'
X%%PYTHON_SITELIBDIR%%/PyProtocols-%%PYPROTOCOLSVER%%-py%%PYTHONVER%%-freebsd-%%EGGOSREL%%-%%ARCH%%.egg
END-of-/usr/ports/devel/py-protocols-devel/pkg-plist
exit
